body:
# Release Notes — v1.16.0 ## New Features & Enhancements ### XYZ — Automatic IAM role translation (AWS & GCP) We've enhanced IAM role mapping for AWS and GCP imports, making the process more accurate and seamless. XYZ maps roles to the appropriate RBAC permissions with minimal user intervention, while clearly highlighting any permissions that require review. - Imported roles show a **Needs Review `(i)`** chip when XYZ detects cloud actions it couldn't translate. - You can view those untranslated actions grouped by service to make review easier. ### XYZ — Azure cost-anomaly detection at Resource Group and Tenant level Cost anomalies on Azure can now be detected at the **Resource Group** and **Tenant** levels, not just the Subscription level. - The Azure notification drawer now shows all three detection levels — Subscription, Resource Group, and Tenant. - The Tenant level stays locked (with an explanatory tooltip) until a tenant hierarchy is connected. - Drawer behaviour is more reliable too: per-account toggles are now independent, severity changes apply cleanly when you downgrade, and Resource Group options only appear when an Azure account is subscribed. ### XYZ — 13 new orphan/idle cleanup rules with auto-remediation Thirteen more orphaned or idle resources can now be detected and cleaned up automatically, end to end: - **AWS** — Secrets Manager secret, KMS key, Internet Gateway, VPC peering, CodeCommit repo, WAF Web ACL, CloudWatch dashboard, Bedrock custom model, Bedrock guardrail. - **Azure** — NSG, VNet peering, Key Vault, public IP. Free / zero-cost orphans (such as Internet Gateways, peerings, NSGs, Key Vaults, secrets, and dashboards) are now flagged correctly — previously they were skipped even when XYZ could see them sitting unused. ### XYZ — Clearer cloud-account permission states The cloud-accounts page now tells you exactly what state your permissions are in, with guided fixes. - Three new states: **Probe-blocked** (XYZ can't even read which permissions you've granted), **Write access not set up** (read/write tier with no write permissions granted), and **Read-only** (the drawer shows read-only features only). - Each state shows an amber banner with a clear count of affected accounts ("N cloud accounts could be missing out"), a chip explaining the state, and step-by-step fix instructions for AWS, Azure, and GCP. - **Recommendation Auto-Remediation** is now its own feature card, separate from Recommendations, so you can see at a glance which one is gated by permissions. ### XYZ Day — Deploy to AWS VMs, with domains, HTTPS, and private registries XYZ Day can now provision a complete **AWS VM** environment (networking, identity, security groups, EC2, and a load balancer) and deploy your git- or image-based services onto it — bringing VMs to parity with clusters and datastores. - Supports custom domains, HTTPS, and private registries. - Multiple services can run on the same VM without disrupting each other's routing. ## Fixes ### Scheduled starts no longer silently skipped A scheduled start could previously be skipped, leaving a resource stopped indefinitely. This happened when a resource was stopped outside XYZ after an earlier XYZ start. Starts are now always issued, so a resource won't get stuck in a stopped state. ### Terminated EMR Serverless apps no longer shown as Active Terminated EMR Serverless apps used to appear as Active and schedulable. XYZ now reads their real state directly and excludes terminated apps, while idle (Created) apps get a working Start toggle. ### Managed-cluster worker nodes no longer offered for scheduling Individual Kubernetes worker nodes (GKE/EKS/AKS) and Auto Scaling Group members were appearing in the schedule picker even though they can't be scheduled on their own (a node pool just recreates a stopped node). They're now hidden — the cluster or node pool itself is still listed. ### ECS Fargate services now show their real cost ECS Fargate services were showing US$0.00 in Cost Reports. They're now priced correctly based on vCPU and memory, while EC2-backed and other compute stays at $0 so nothing is double-counted. ### Cost anomaly details — missing metadata and misleading root-cause messages - Cloud Account and Region now show for terminated/ephemeral resources (e.g. Karpenter EKS nodes) in cost-anomaly details. - Fixed a misleading "hourly rate changed" message for scheduled resources that were simply restarted after being stopped — these now correctly show as resuming spend after a period of $0. ### UI & Experience Improvements - **Consistent hover hints** — styled tooltips across the app, shown only when text is actually clipped, with screen-reader labels on icon-only controls. - **Cost Over Time from day 1** — shows a "collecting" summary (MTD total + per-day mini-bars) with 1–6 days of data; the full chart appears from day 7. - **Create Override dialog** — sensible default expiry (24h), lists any missing required fields, and auto-selects the only eligible resource/group. - **Mobile sidebar** — page scroll is locked while the drawer is open and restored on close. ## Technical details
IAM Import — translator widening + "View details" surface The translator no longer drops a whole role just because one statement has a Condition. **AWS:** per-statement skip for conditional / `NotAction` Allows — the actions inside still translate; `needsReview` is set so the admin reviews. Hard bail only for conditional / `NotAction` Deny statements (where ignoring the restriction would silently widen the grant). Generic sub-service prefix fallback (`ec2-instance-connect`, `s3-object-lambda`, `eks-auth`, …) auto-routes to the catalog prefix's XYZ resource type via longest-prefix match. `elasticloadbalancing:*` fans out to alb + clb + gwlb + nlb full CRUD. **GCP:** service-level fallback handles unknown two-segment prefixes whose first segment is a single-primary-type service (`container`, `storage`, `bigquery`, `pubsub`, `cloudsql`, `dataflow`, …). New compound verb-prefix rule table classifies read-class (`get*` / `list*` / `view*` → view), write-class (`set*` / `add*` / `attach*` / `start*` / `stop*` → update), and `delete*` / `purge*` → delete. `iam.serviceAccounts.actAs/signBlob/signJwt/...` covered as platform actions. `expandResourceCRUDHierarchy` always expands to full CRUD on any write donor — the 4-verb XYZ model can't represent partial AWS Deny, so the honest signal is `needsReview = denySubtractedWriteVerb`. `TranslationMetadata.NeedsReview` is no longer `omitempty` so clean re-imports overwrite stale `true → false` instead of silently merging away. `stampRoleMetadata` uses JSON `MergeRoleMetadata` so cross-account `linkedFrom` entries survive re-imports. `role.Store.ClearTranslationMarker` extracted from inline SQL so the service layer stays out of the store. Frontend: `UntranslatedActionsModal` uses the shared `Modal` primitive (16+ existing call-sites), `InfoTooltip` converted from CSS group-hover to Floating UI `safePolygon` so the cursor can travel from the icon into the popup to reach the CTA. Matrix collapses every category to a single union row (broad ∪ typed); typed-view chip suppressed when broad-view is granted (fixes duplicate `Resource` rows on roles like `appengine.appCreator`). `appCreator` curated mapping trimmed to stop over-granting view on every category, pinned with a regression test.
Azure hierarchy anomaly detection (aggregator) Two new Go detectors in `aggregator/anomaly_azure_hierarchy.go`: `azureResourceGroupDetector` (parses RG name from ARM UID via `ParseAzureResourceGroup` in `cost/derive_account.go` — case-insensitive segment match on `/resourceGroups/{rg}/`) and `azureTenantDetector` (groups subscriptions via Config gRPC `parent_account_id`). `client/config_grpc.go` `ListCloudAccounts` extended with `include_children: true` filter for the tenant hierarchy walk; `CloudAccountInfo` gains `ID`, `Scope`, `ParentAccountID`. `anomaly.go` prefetches cloud accounts and auto-includes Azure detectors when a cloud_account is subscribed; `collectGoDetectorUIDs` is case-insensitive against ARM UID casing. `anomaly_sql.go` restored `resource_type` in the SQL dimension mapping (accidentally removed in an earlier change) plus tests. Frontend: notification drawer for Azure shows three detection levels — Subscription / Resource Group / Tenant — with Tenant locked + tooltip when no tenant hierarchy is connected; entity-scoped subscriptions correctly disabled on severity downgrade; per-account and per-level toggles isolated.
Orphan auto-remediation — discoverer + recommender + provisioner 13 rules wired end-to-end. **Discoverer** new providers: Internet Gateway (attachment state), WAFv2 Web ACL (association count), CodeCommit (last-commit activity). VPC peering now emits dead-state (rejected / expired / failed) connections instead of dropping them. VNet peering disconnected peerings emitted as standalone resources keyed by ARM id. NSG association state computed from subnets/NICs already in the Resource Graph response (no extra API call). Adds `wavfv2` + `codecommit` AWS SDK deps. **Recommender** bug fixes: free / usage-priced orphans (IGW, peering, NSG, Key Vault, Secrets, dashboard, Bedrock) were suppressed by a `cost<=0` gate and never fired — now fire on the orphan state at $0. Rules read their orphan-state flag from resource Metadata only — a Tags fallback was deliberately not used (a customer tag like `attached=false` would otherwise be misread as the orphan signal). `<$5` low-savings suppression now exempts the orphan category only; idle / rightsizing / discount / schedule unchanged. **Provisioner** new delete adapters: AWS — IGW, WAF (`GetWebACL` → `DeleteWebACL`), CodeCommit, VPC peering, KMS, Secrets Manager, CloudWatch dashboard, Bedrock custom-model/guardrail; Azure — Key Vault, NSG, Public IP, VNet peering. All idempotent on not-found; WAF/IGW surface a typed user-action error when still associated/attached.
Cloud-account permission states UI (XYZ + XYZ Day) Three states: **probe-blocked** (XYZ can't read which permissions are granted) — amber banner, card chip "Feature status unknown", drawer copy explaining the situation, what you'll see once access is granted (provider-aware: AWS/Azure role vs GCP Cloud Resource Manager API), and step-by-step how-to-fix; **write access not set up** (read_write tier, no write perms granted) — card chip + drawer notice + banner bullet routing to a Fix drawer that creates the complete write policy; **read-only** — drawer filters to read features only. Recommendation Auto-Remediation split out from Recommendations as its own feature card. Catalog: `docs/permissions/XYZ/*.json` destructive/write perms moved from `recommendations` to a new `remediation` feature; `permissionscatalog` (config + discoverer) adds `remediation` to valid XYZ features. `Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/listClusterAdminCredential/action` removed from the Azure catalog — AKS discovery fetches its kubeconfig via `ListClusterUserCredentials` (least privilege); the discoverer keeps `ListClusterAdminCredentials` only as an opportunistic best-effort fallback (`aks_kube_client.go` / `aks_workloads.go`) that degrades gracefully when not granted.
Executor — drop stale state_history pre-check `skipIfAlreadyInDesiredState` read `GetLatestResourceState` — the executor's own `state_history` (the last action it issued) — as a proxy for actual cloud state, and skipped the provider call when it matched. When the two diverge (resource stopped outside XYZ after a XYZ start, so `state_history` still says `started`), the start was wrongly skipped. Starts are now issued unconditionally (`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: "No state pre-check. No discoverer dependency. Intent is self-contained" — EC2 `StartInstances` on a running instance is an idempotent no-op). Adds no new inter-service/gRPC calls. Stops keep a narrow `skipIfAlreadyStopped` guard for the scale-to-zero provider family (ASG, ECS, EKS, Beanstalk, Lambda, k8s & GKE workloads, Azure Batch/ML/Databricks-pool) — these are not idempotent across repeated stops: `Stop` snapshots the live size and `Start` restores it, with no guard against being already at zero, so a repeated `Stop` snapshots `0` and clobbers the restore target. Sibling stale-state guard in `handler.go` for manual bulk/group actions is tracked as a separate follow-up.
EMR Serverless — dedicated provider replaces Resource Explorer hardcoded state EMR Serverless was discovered through AWS Resource Explorer, which returns an ARN/type/region but no application state — the discoverer hardcoded `Status = "active"` for every Resource Explorer result, so `TERMINATED` apps surfaced as live, schedulable resources and never self-healed. New `EMRServerlessProvider` (`emr_serverless.go`) calls `emr-serverless:ListApplications` with an active-states filter (`CREATING / CREATED / STARTING / STARTED / STOPPING / STOPPED`) — excludes `TERMINATED`. Registered in `main.go` as `aws-emr-serverless` and added to the Resource Explorer `SkipTypes` so the generic index no longer double-counts it (no per-type state logic leaks into the generic Resource Explorer). `emr-serverless:ListApplications` is included in the AWS `ReadOnlyAccess` managed policy — no new discovery permission required. Frontend `ResourcesPage.jsx` adds `'created'` to the stable-states set so EMR Serverless's idle `CREATED` state renders an enabled Start toggle.
Managed-cluster nodes filtered from the schedule picker (`applySchedulable`) `applySchedulable` in `backend/discoverer/internal/service/service.go` set `Schedulable` purely from resource type. `ec2` / `compute` / `vm` are schedulable types, so managed-pool member nodes were marked schedulable and surfaced in the picker. Fix: additionally demote a raw VM (`ec2` / `compute` / `vm`) to `Schedulable=false` when it has a non-empty `ParentUID`. A raw VM is given a `ParentUID` only when it belongs to a managed group (GKE/Dataproc labels, EKS/ASG/ECS/EMR/Karpenter tags, GCP MIG `created-by`, AKS pool tag) — a standalone, individually-stoppable VM never has a parent. No parent-resource lookup, no cross-batch dependency, no metadata parsing. Group/cluster types stay schedulable (`asg`, `eks-nodegroup`, `eks-cluster`, `gke-nodepool`, `gke-cluster`, `aks-agentpool`, `aks-cluster`, `vmss`). Already-stored managed nodes on other accounts keep `schedulable=1` until their next discovery sync — a backfill is intentionally deferred to a follow-up PR that also detaches affected `schedule_resources` rows, because a bare `UPDATE` would strand any existing schedule attached to the demoted nodes (the Schedules tab hides but the evaluator's `buildResourceMap` does not filter on `schedulable`).
App-wide Tooltip sweep + Cost Trend / Override / Mobile sidebar fixes 153 native `title=` attributes across 58 files converted to ``: truncated text → `onlyOnOverflow`, informational hints → always-on, icon-only controls also get an `aria-label`. Left untouched: `
Drop legacy `cloud_account_permissions` table (discoverer) Phase 2 of the permission lifecycle. The `cloud_account_permissions` table was superseded by the per-permission `permissions` table (#1409 / #1554) and is no longer read or written by any code. New migration `20260603000000_drop_cloud_account_permissions` registered in `migrations/all.go`; `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS`. Stale comment in `service.go` fixed (referred to the old table; the `errMsgMax` / `truncateErrMsg` code now serves the `permissions` table). Old create/alter migrations intentionally kept as immutable history.
ECS Fargate cost — launch-type resolution + FARGATE pricing whitelist (discoverer + aggregator) Every `ecs-service` row computed $0. `calcECSService` priced a service only when its launch type wasn't `EC2`/empty, and the discoverer left `InstanceType` empty for capacity-provider services — modern Fargate declares a `capacityProviderStrategy`, so `Service.LaunchType` is `""` and the cluster fallback emitted lower-case that slipped past the `== "EC2"` guard. **Discoverer:** new `ecsServiceLaunchType` helper resolves the launch type — `Service.LaunchType` → capacity-provider strategy (`FARGATE`/`FARGATE_SPOT` ⇒ FARGATE, any other provider ⇒ EC2) → cluster fallback — and emits a canonical upper-case value; `mapECSService` now stores the resolved value in `metadata.launch_type` instead of the empty raw field. **Aggregator:** `calcECSService` normalizes case and prices only `FARGATE`; every other launch type (EC2, EXTERNAL/ECS-Anywhere, MANAGED_INSTANCES, the "mixed" cluster fallback, empty) returns $0, so EC2-backed / on-prem compute is never double-counted against its instances and new SDK enum values default to safe. Code-only, no migration — the aggregator heals existing stored rows on the next aggregation cycle. (#1587)
